steps.title: Identify the structure and conditions for intersection
steps.content: We have two linear equations in slope-intercept form: $y = x + 23$ and $y = 12x$. Two distinct non-parallel lines intersect at exactly one point, while parallel lines do not intersect at all.

steps.title: Compare slopes to check for parallel lines
steps.content: From the equations, the first line has slope 1 and the second has slope 12. Since $1 \neq 12$, the slopes are different, so the lines are not parallel. Therefore they must intersect at exactly one point. That matches choice B — B is correct.
